summary: Preserves six validated byte-exact fixes made in the previous campaign by applying them only inside archive/previous_campaign, without touching the active dtk-template source units or objdiff progress numbers.
decomp_lessons: Small C-shape rewrites can flip a near-miss to byte-exact: replacing a static forward declaration with an extern one (when the function is defined non-static later in the same TU) changed codegen enough to matter for __pformatter.
Explicit narrowing casts to u16 (instead of relying on implicit truncation) can match MWCC's register-narrowing behavior exactly.

Extracting a literal into a named local variable, or wrapping a statement in an explicit block scope, can nudge MWCC's register allocation/stack layout to match the target even when the logic is unchanged.

Archive-only changes (files under archive/previous_campaign) don't affect active build/progress and can be validated independently via a git merge-tree dry run against master to prove a clean, non-conflicting merge.
smart_moves: Validated the PR wouldn't disturb the active tree by running git diff --check and a git merge-tree dry run against origin/master before merging, plus a scan confirming no .inc files were introduced.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Replaced 'static s32 __pformatter(...)' forward-decl with 'extern s32 __pformatter(...)' while keeping the same later definition, changing the compiler's calling-convention/linkage assumptions to match target.
Cast vertCount arithmetic in fn_800D6A80 (gs_render.c) to (u16) at each accumulation step to match target register truncation for 16-bit vertex/primitive counts.
Hoisted the dereferenced base address of lbl_8047AA80 into a local 'base' variable in fn_800D75F4/fn_800D7650 to match the compiler's expected load scheduling.

key_files.role: __pformatter forward-decl changed from static to extern to match target linkage/codegen
Added explicit u16 casts and hoisted base-address locals in fn_800D6A80/fn_800D75F4/fn_800D7650 to match register behavior
Wrapped isActive assignment in a block-scoped local to match compiler stack layout
